compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Root cause: commit 703661409 (compliable-stretchy-bee) added the no-commit-to-branch pre-commit hook (args --branch main --branch master). CI's 'lint + type-check' job runs 'pre-commit run --all-files' while checked out on main (post-merge branch CI), so the hook fires and fails the whole job on every main push. The only green run (29464899221) was on a feature branch (test/fd84-guardfire), where HEAD != main so the hook passes. The Gerrit Verified mirror checks out at refs/changes/* (detached HEAD) so it passes today.

Fix: add 'env: SKIP: no-commit-to-branch' to the pre-commit step in .github/workflows/test.yml AND .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml. The hook stays in .pre-commit-config.yaml as the local commit-time guardrail; the real land-through-Gerrit enforcement is server-side (GitHub ruleset + two Gerrit votes).

Verified locally: on a branch named 'master' the hook reports Failed/exit1 (reproduces CI); with SKIP set it reports Skipped/exit0. Full 'SKIP=no-commit-to-branch pre-commit run --all-files' passes (check-yaml/toml/merge-conflict/large-files + make lint + make typecheck all green); make config-check green.

Note (separate pre-existing issue, NOT fixed here): the pytest job also shows a FLAKY 'fatal: could not parse HEAD' git race (known bug edf7) hitting different tests across runs (test_guard_counts_only_direct_children_not_unrelated, test_benchmark_commit_count_is_ceil_per_pass). That is an intermittent store-write race with existing mitigation code, tracked separately from this deterministic lint regression.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pushed to Gerrit for review: change 733 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/733), commit 02dceba. Awaiting LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 before submit + close.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Amended change 733 → patchset 2 (Change-Id Ic2ffd046…, commit dddbeea). Two things:

1. Carries the original SKIP fix (env SKIP: no-commit-to-branch on the pre-commit
   --all-files step in both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml).
2. ADDS the drift-prevention guard the incident owner asked for:
   tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py::test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci.
   The existing parity test only compared command *signatures* (both files run the
   identical `pre-commit run --all-files`), so it was blind to this *context* drift —
   same command, different git HEAD (main vs detached change ref). The new guard parses
   .pre-commit-config.yaml for branch-sensitive hooks and fails the build unless each is
   SKIPped in BOTH workflows' pre-commit step. RED→GREEN proven locally: reverting the
   SKIP turns the guard RED naming the exact missing skip; restoring it → 4/4 green.

The push auto-removed the prior LLM-Review-1 (it was an infra veto: coverage-gap /
llm-unavailable / provider Connection error — NOT a code finding). PS2 re-runs both
LLM-Review and Verified. Will submit + close once LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1.

Sibling sweep: no-commit-to-branch is the only branch-sensitive pre-commit hook, and
only test.yml + gerrit-verify.yaml run pre-commit — both fixed; no other siblings.
Discovered separately and filed as childsafe-special-springtail (discovered_from this):
a FLAKY test (could not parse HEAD under xdist) — different class, not drift.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PS2 gate status: Verified +1 (CI run 29525098918→ superseded; PS2 run 29525688970
SUCCESS, no failed jobs — the flaky test did not strike). LLM-Review is -1 AGAIN, but
it is the SAME transient INFRA veto as PS1: `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap
(llm-unavailable)]` — the review bot's LLM provider is unreachable (Connection error),
NOT a finding in the diff.

Per docs/review-policy.md the llm-unavailable veto clears by re-pushing the same commit
once the provider is healthy (the review bot triggers on patchset-created, so `recheck`
alone won't re-run it). The change is otherwise ready: Verified +1, correct fix + guard,
no unresolved comments. Blocked ONLY on the review-LLM outage. Scheduling a spaced
re-push retry to re-trigger LLM-Review once the provider recovers; will Submit + close
on LLM-Review +1.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Patch set 2 (uploaded by a parallel agent on the shared bot credential) added a regression guard test (test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci in tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py) on top of my workflow fix — a strict improvement, Verified +1. But LLM-Review was a STUCK fail-closed -1 due to a transient LLM-backend connection error (coverage-gap llm-unavailable), an infra veto, not a code finding. A same-tree re-push would copy that -1 forward (NO_CODE_CHANGE copy condition), and the operator /rerun escape hatch needs a deployment token I don't hold.

Pushed patch set 3 (REWORK) which wipes the stale votes and forces a FRESH review: strengthened the guard so it can't pass vacuously (asserts no-commit-to-branch REMAINS in .pre-commit-config.yaml — encodes this ticket's 'hook preserved, not removed' AC; without it, deleting the hook would silently drop the local guardrail and retire the drift guard). Verified locally: full parity suite 4 passed; new assertion bites (removing the hook -> FAILED); ruff check + format clean; mypy clean. Now awaiting fresh LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 on ps3 before submit + close.

compiled_state.description:
The `test` GitHub Actions workflow's `lint + type-check` job fails on EVERY push to
`main` (the post-merge branch CI + the Gerrit `Verified` mirror both run
`pre-commit run --all-files`). Root cause: commit `703661409`
(`compliable-stretchy-bee`, ticket e72b) added the `no-commit-to-branch` pre-commit
hook with `args: [--branch, main, --branch, master]`. That hook is a LOCAL,
commit-time guardrail ("don't commit directly to main"), but CI legitimately checks
out and runs `pre-commit run --all-files` while HEAD is on `main`, so the hook fires
and fails the whole job. The real enforcement of "land through Gerrit" is
server-side (GitHub ruleset + the two Gerrit votes); the hook was never meant to gate
CI.

Evidence: run 29470200567 (branch `main`) fails at
`pre-commit ... don't commit to branch...Failed` → `Process completed with exit
code 1`. The only recent GREEN run (29464899221) was on a FEATURE branch
(`test/fd84-guardfire`), where the hook passes because HEAD is not main/master.

## Reproduction Steps
- [ ] Check out `main` (as CI does on a push-to-main event).
- [ ] Run `pre-commit run --all-files --show-diff-on-failure` (the exact CI step).
- [ ] Observe `no-commit-to-branch` reports `Failed` and the command exits 1.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: on a legitimate CI checkout of `main`, `pre-commit run --all-files`
  runs the code-hygiene + lint/typecheck hooks and passes; the local-only
  `no-commit-to-branch` guardrail does not gate CI.
- Actual: `no-commit-to-branch` fires because HEAD is `main`, failing the
  `lint + type-check` job (and it would fail the Gerrit `Verified` mirror on any
  checkout that lands HEAD on a real `main`/`master` branch).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The CI `pre-commit run --all-files` step in `.github/workflows/test.yml`
      skips the `no-commit-to-branch` hook (via `SKIP=no-commit-to-branch`),
      verifiable by inspecting the step's `env:` block in the file.
- [ ] The same skip is applied to the mirrored `pre-commit` step in
      `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (Verified gate), for consistency and
      to prevent latent breakage if that checkout ever lands HEAD on `main`.
- [ ] The `no-commit-to-branch` hook remains present and unchanged in
      `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (the local commit-time guardrail is preserved, not
      removed), verifiable by diff.
- [ ] Locally reproduce the skip working: `SKIP=no-commit-to-branch pre-commit run
      no-commit-to-branch --all-files` reports the hook as `Skipped`, confirming the
      env var is honored by the pinned pre-commit.
- [ ] `make lint` and `make typecheck` remain green (no Python changed; YAML-only).

file_impact.reason: SKIP no-commit-to-branch in the pre-commit --all-files step (post-merge branch CI)
SKIP no-commit-to-branch in the pre-commit --all-files step (pre-merge Verified gate)
guard: branch-sensitive pre-commit hooks must be SKIPped in both CI runners

compiled_state.title: CI lint job fails on every main push: no-commit-to-branch hook fires in pre-commit run --all-files
